notes: This is a relatively easy UI text-extraction task and most models (8 of 15) scored a perfect 10 by transcribing all visible text verbatim and correctly describing the unchecked checkbox, dot-masked password, red error box, and blue button.

The three most common failure modes were: (a) mislabeling the middle macOS traffic-light button as 'orange' instead of yellow (gemma4-12b, gemma4-e4b); (b) representing the password as asterisks '********' instead of dots (qwen3-vl-4b, qwen3-vl-8b, qwen3.5-9b|nothink); and (c) outright fabrication of duplicate elements (gemma4-e4b invented a second error box and a second 'Forgot your password?' link).

The two most severe failures were both Gemma models: gemma4-e4b|think hallucinated duplicated UI elements (score 4) and gemma4-26b-a4b|think claimed the page background was dark/black when it is light gray (score 6). Both retained correct text transcription, so the failures are purely perceptual/structural.

No model failed to extract any required text string — every response included 'Sign in to Acme', 'user@example.com', 'Keep me signed in', 'Invalid credentials. Please try again.', 'Sign In', and 'Forgot your password?' verbatim.

The Qwen3.6 family (27b and 35b-a3b, both think and nothink) produced the most consistently detailed and accurate responses, frequently including correct hex color codes for the traffic-light buttons.
